policy.description: Blocking line-coverage ratchets. Update measured counts in the same reviewed change whenever scope or coverage advances; never lower a floor without an explicit, justified scope change.

tolerance.rationale: Two clean local Node 22/V8 coverage runs on 2026-07-17 passed 1244 tests, and the final clean run passed 1249 tests; all three measured the identical complete 2253-line core scope at 1932 covered lines (85.752%). Issue #122's localized/late home-row regressions exercise existing navigation and action-sheet paths, advancing 17 covered lines over the reviewed 1915/2253 baseline without changing scope. The existing one-line tolerance is unchanged and permits only the previously reproduced negligible instrumentation variance; coverage-baseline.test.js retains exact below-floor, above-baseline, scope-change, and broad-tolerance negative boundaries.

Theme Studio responsive editor hardening in issue #386. Two clean local .NET 10.0.9/Coverlet runs on 2026-07-19 each passed all 1822 tests over the identical complete 29168-line assembly scope and measured 20726 covered lines. Relative to issue #387's 20709/29150 baseline, the legitimate 18-line scope increase preserves dormant schedules while administrator scheduling is disabled, rejects new scheduled imports, and adds exact controller coverage for both capability paths; 17 of the 18 new instrumented lines are covered, improving total assembly coverage from 71.043% to 71.057%. The existing four-line low-side tolerance is unchanged, continues to span the previously reproduced two-line Coverlet concurrency variance, and remains far below the policy maximum; coverage-baseline.test.js retains the exact scope-drift, regression, stale-baseline, and broad-tolerance negative boundaries.
